title: PMKVY 4.0 — Skill India Free Training & Certification
meta_description: PMKVY 4.0 2026 — free skill training in 200+ trades with government certification. Stipend during training, placement assistance. 1.6 crore trained since 2015.

hero.one_liner: Free skill training and certification in 200+ trades — from IT to plumbing to beauty to accounting — with stipend and placement assistance

what_is_it: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is India's flagship skill training scheme under the Skill India Mission. It provides free short-term training (200-600 hours) in over 200 trades and job roles, along with government-recognized certification, a training stipend, and placement assistance. The scheme is now in its 4th phase (PMKVY 4.0), running from 2022 to 2026.

PMKVY 4.0 focuses on new-age skills like Industry 4.0, Web 3.0, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, AR/VR, climate change solutions, and green economy jobs. As of December 2025, over 27 lakh candidates have been trained under PMKVY 4.0 across 38 sectors. Since 2015, the scheme has certified 1.29 crore candidates out of 1.64 crore trained.

Training is provided at PMKVY Training Centers (TCs) and Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendras (PMKKs) spread across every district in India. Courses cover sectors like IT-ITeS, retail, hospitality, healthcare, construction, automotive, beauty & wellness, agriculture, electronics, and many more. You can search training centers at pmkvyofficial.org or the Skill India Digital Hub.

After completing training and passing the assessment, candidates receive a National Skills Qualifications Framework (NSQF) aligned certificate — recognized by employers across India. Many training centers also provide placement assistance, connecting graduates with relevant employers. Studies show that 94% of employers prefer PMKVY-trained candidates.

PMKVY also has a Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) component — if you already have skills through informal work experience (like a carpenter or tailor who learned on the job), you can get your existing skills assessed and certified without going through full training. RPL candidates receive an honorarium of ₹500 upon certification.

eligibility.label: Education
eligibility.value: Varies by course — some require no formal education, others need 8th/10th/12th pass

eligibility.label: Fee
eligibility.value: Completely FREE — no training fee, assessment fee, or certification fee

eligibility.label: Anyone can join
eligibility.value: Students, unemployed youth, school dropouts, workers wanting to upskill — no income restriction

extra_sections.heading: Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL)
content:
What is RPL?

Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) is a pathway for working professionals and those with informal skills to get certified without attending full training. If you've been doing a job informally for years — as a welder, plumber, tailor, or electrician — RPL lets you skip training and go straight to assessment and certification.

Who is RPL for?

RPL is ideal for workers in unregulated sectors who learned skills on the job. This includes self-employed individuals, informal workers, and those changing careers.
You must have at least basic prior experience in your chosen job role. RPL is open to candidates aged 15-45 with Aadhaar, a bank account, and relevant work experience.

How does RPL work?

The process has 4 steps: (1) Counselling & Pre-screening — you meet advisors and take a pre-screening test to confirm your skills match a job role.
(2) Orientation — 12 hours of training covering 6 hours domain knowledge, 4 hours soft skills & entrepreneurship, and 2 hours assessment familiarization.

(3) Assessment — third-party assessors test your skills across both core and non-core job competencies. (4) Certification — if you score 50% (NSQF levels 1-3) or 70% (levels 4+), you get your NSQF certificate.

What certificate do you get?

You receive an NSQF (National Skills Qualifications Framework) certificate identical to the regular PMKVY certificate. It's recognized by employers across India and is equivalent to an ITI qualification for many roles.
You also receive a marksheet, candidate payslip, and ₹500 honorarium upon certification.

extra_sections.heading: PMKVY 4.0 vs Previous Versions
rows: Full digitalization via Skill India Digital Hub

extra_sections.heading: What Happens After Training
content:
Placement Rates & Job Prospects

PMKVY 4.0 does not guarantee placement, but placement assistance is provided. Training centers connect you with employers in relevant sectors.

According to earlier data (PMKVY 1.0-3.0), placement rates averaged 40-50% with significant variation by sector. IT, retail, and healthcare sectors show higher placement success (50-70%), while some construction roles have lower rates.

Certificate Value in the Job Market

Your NSQF certificate is recognized across India by both government and private employers. Studies show 94% of surveyed employers prefer PMKVY candidates.
The certificate is equivalent to ITI qualifications for many job roles. Some employers treat PMKVY graduates similarly to diploma holders depending on the sector.
Many roles in retail, hospitality, healthcare, and IT actively recruit PMKVY pass-outs.

Your Next Steps After Certification

Immediate (within 1 month): Attend placement drives organized by your training center. Update your CV with your NSQF certificate.

Register on Skill India Digital Hub for job opportunities. Reach out to industry connections suggested by your center.

Medium-term (1-3 months): If not placed, consider apprenticeships or internships in your field.

Pursue additional certifications to increase competitiveness. Use your stipend (if received) to invest in tools or further training.

Long-term (3-12 months): Explore self-employment or entrepreneurship in your field.
Consider upskilling in related areas to increase earning potential. Transition to permanent roles from temporary assignments.
Some PMKVY graduates go on to start their own businesses (plumbing, salon, repair shops, etc.).

how_to_apply.title: Visit pmkvyofficial.org or Skill India Digital portal
how_to_apply.description: Search for training centers near you. Filter by sector, job role, and location. You can also call the helpline at 088000-55555 for assistance in finding centers.

how_to_apply.title: Visit the training center and enroll
how_to_apply.description: Carry Aadhaar card, educational certificates, bank passbook, and passport photos. The center will verify your eligibility and enroll you. No fee is charged for registration.

how_to_apply.title: Complete training and pass assessment
how_to_apply.description: Attend classes regularly (usually 2-6 months depending on course). After completion, take the assessment conducted by a third-party agency approved by Sector Skill Councils. Pass marks are 50% for NSQF levels 1-3 and 70% for level 4+.

how_to_apply.title: Get placement assistance and start career
how_to_apply.description: Training centers connect you with employers through placement drives and interviews. Your NSQF certificate and digital profile on Skill India are your credentials. Centers may also provide entrepreneurship guidance if you prefer self-employment.

important_alert.text: PMKVY training is 100% FREE. If any center asks for money for training, assessment, or certification, it's unauthorized. Report immediately at the helpline 088000-55555 or at pmkvyofficial.org. Only ₹500 candidate honorarium (for RPL) is government-provided cash.

important_dates.label: Enrollment
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year — rolling admissions

faqs.question: Will I get a job after PMKVY training?
faqs.answer: PMKVY provides placement ASSISTANCE, not guaranteed placement. Training centers connect you with employers in the relevant sector. Placement rates vary by sector and location — IT, retail, and healthcare have higher rates (50-70%). The NSQF certificate significantly improves your employability compared to having no certification. Studies show 94% of employers prefer PMKVY candidates.

faqs.question: I'm 35 and already working. Can I join?
faqs.answer: Yes. PMKVY is open up to age 45. If you want to upskill or learn a new trade, you're eligible. The Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) component is specifically designed for working adults — get your existing skills certified without leaving your job.

faqs.question: Is the PMKVY certificate recognized by private companies?
faqs.answer: Yes. The NSQF (National Skills Qualifications Framework) certificate is recognized across India by both government and private employers. It's aligned with industry standards and is equivalent to an ITI certificate for many job roles.

faqs.question: Can I do PMKVY twice in different sectors?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can enroll in multiple PMKVY courses in different sectors across your lifetime. However, each course must be completed separately with its own assessment. Some centers may have specific policies — check with your local training center for details.

faqs.question: Is online training available under PMKVY?
faqs.answer: PMKVY 4.0 emphasizes in-person practical training since most skills require hands-on learning. However, the Skill India Digital Hub provides online modules for orientation, soft skills, and theory components. Practical training and assessments must be completed at registered training centers.

faqs.question: How do I verify if a training center is genuine?
faqs.answer: Visit pmkvyofficial.org and search for registered centers in your location. Genuine centers will have government recognition displayed. Your center should have proper facilities, qualified instructors, and be registered with Sector Skill Councils. If unsure, call the helpline 088000-55555 to verify the center's authenticity.

faqs.question: What's the difference between PMKVY and ITI?
faqs.answer: ITIs provide longer training (6 months to 3 years) in 126 trades with a 70% theory, 30% practical mix. PMKVY offers shorter, focused training (2-6 months) in 200+ trades with 70% practical, 30% theory focus. PMKVY is faster and more employment-focused; ITI is more comprehensive. ITI graduates report ~63.5% placement rates; PMKVY candidates have showed higher employer preference (94%). Choose PMKVY for quick upskilling, ITI for deeper technical knowledge.

faqs.question: Do I need a bank account to enroll in PMKVY?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll need an active bank account (preferably linked to Aadhaar) for receiving stipends, candidate honorarium, and placements. You can open a basic savings account at any bank free of cost.

ministry: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship
